title: erysimum incanum subsp. matritense
intro: Erysimum incanum subsp. matritense, also known as Matritense Wallflower, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and slender foliage. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Erysimum incanum subsp. matritense are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, suggesting its potential in treating various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
